title: Saint John's Day
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## September 8, 2019 | Preacher: Richard Lawson

---

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

September 8, 2019 was the Fourteenth Sunday after Pentecost in the Christian calendar, falling within Ordinary Time. Under the Revised Common Lectionary (RCL), the readings would have included Proverbs 22:1-2, 8-9, 16-17a; Psalm 125; James 2:1-10 (11-13) 14-17; and Mark 7:24-37, with themes around faith, favoritism, and healing. The feast of the Nativity of Mary (September 8 in some traditions) may have held additional liturgical significance depending on Saint John's Cathedral's observance practices.

EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the period following General Convention 2018 (Austin, Texas), implementing resolutions on climate action, racial reconciliation, and expanded marriage canons. The Diocese of Colorado was under the leadership of Bishop Robert Wright (who served 2013-2022), and the cathedral itself was undergoing its identity as the seat of the diocese. No major ecclesiastical controversies or bishop elections were imminent at this specific moment, though the broader Anglican Communion continued debates on human sexuality and church authority.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was in the final weeks of summer 2019 with typical September weather patterns—warm days, cooling nights, low humidity. City politics centered on Mayor Michael Hancock's administration and ongoing development in downtown Denver and Capitol Hill. The Colorado legislature was not in session (off-year from the 2019 regular session), so state politics were relatively quiet. No major disasters or immediate crises dominated local headlines in early September 2019.

NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The Trump administration was navigating trade tensions with China, with tariff negotiations ongoing and the stock market showing volatility. Hurricane Dorian was approaching the U.S. East Coast (making landfall in the Carolinas on September 5-6), dominating national weather news. The 2020 presidential campaign was intensifying, with Democratic primary debates having occurred in late July/early August and candidates visiting key early states. Wildfires continued to affect the American West, including Colorado. The cultural moment reflected ongoing political polarization and climate anxiety.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The U.K. faced political turmoil as Prime Minister Boris Johnson pursued Brexit and suspended Parliament (prorogation) in early September, triggering constitutional debates. Hong Kong was in the midst of major pro-democracy protests and civil unrest following extradition bill disputes. The India-Pakistan tensions over Kashmir remained elevated following the August 5 revocation of Kashmir's special status by the Modi government.

CULTURAL MOMENT

September 2019 reflected a cultural climate of deepening political division in the U.S., climate change anxiety (with Greta Thunberg's climate activism gaining prominence), and uncertainty about 2020 election outcomes. The Denver Broncos season was beginning, providing a cultural focal point for the city. Social media discourse was increasingly polarized, and there was a widespread sense that American institutions were under strain. For an Episcopal congregation, themes of reconciliation, prophetic witness, and Christian unity would have carried particular resonance given the denominational and national contexts.
